facts.text: Wilton Manors was incorporated in 1947 as the Village of Wilton Manors, officially becoming a municipality under Chapter 165 of Florida Statutes.
The city’s name was coined in 1925 by Ned Willingham, a Georgia transplant who developed the area into a planned community.
Wilton Manors’ first library opened on June 24, 1957, thanks to a volunteer project by the Jayceettes; the city took over the library the following year.
The city’s largest recreational spots include Hagen Park, Richardson Historic Park and Nature Preserve, and Island City Park Preserve, each offering unique green spaces.
A train stop on the Florida East Coast Railroad near the current NE 24th Street once carried the name Wilton Manors, linking the city to regional rail history.
In 1976 the Wilton Manors Historical Society unveiled a Bicentennial City Quilt, a commemorative piece that showcases the city’s history through stitched images.
